question.text: Under Rule 9 of the PML Rules, what is the beneficial ownership threshold for an unincorporated association or body of individuals?

answer.text: More than 10 percent of property, capital, or profits
More than 15 percent of property, capital, or profits
More than 20 percent of property, capital, or profits
More than 25 percent of property, capital, or profits

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Rule 9(3)(c) states that for an unincorporated association or body of individuals, the beneficial owner is the natural person(s) having ownership of or entitlement to more than 15% of the property, capital, or profits. This is higher than the 10% threshold applicable to companies and partnership firms.
**The Trap:** The threshold differs by entity type: Companies and partnerships = more than 10%; Unincorporated associations = more than 15%. For trusts, there is no percentage threshold -- you must identify the author, trustee, beneficiaries with 10%+ interest, and any person with ultimate effective control.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Series XXIV uses 25% as the threshold under SEBI guidelines (different from IFSCA). The IFSCA exam follows the PMLA/IFSCA Guidelines which use 10% (companies/partnerships) and 15% (unincorporated associations) -- lower thresholds reflecting stricter IFSC standards.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Lower thresholds mean more beneficial owners to identify. For companies: anyone with more than 10%. For unincorporated bodies: anyone with more than 15%. If no one is identified, default to the senior managing official. This is stricter than many other jurisdictions.
